noted_behavior: Mixed baseline: one run around -13 (short-medium episodes), another around -31 (poor performance).
Likely unstable due to high learning rate; rewards inconsistent, episodes terminate quickly.
Strongly negative rewards (~-25 to -30), short episodes; agent fails to learn effective policy.
Moderately negative (~-15 to -19), episodes somewhat longer; lower gamma may reduce stability.
Slight improvement (~-17), longer episodes; high gamma stabilizes but still negative rewards.
Poor performance (~-30), shorter episodes; small batch size hurts stability.
Positive rewards (~+2), longer episodes; larger batch size yields stable and successful learning.
Negative rewards, short episodes; reduced exploration fraction limits learning.
Strongly negative (~-34), unstable episodes; high exploration fraction prevents convergence.
Similar to baseline, negative rewards, medium episodes; no significant improvement.
Dipped to ~-30 during exploration then recovered steadily to -18.5; learns but slowly, curve still rising at cutoff.
Declined monotonically to -35.7 with no recovery; even 5x higher lr destabilizes the Q-updates, so speed cannot come from lr.
Sank to ~-30 then drifted flat at -28; updates too small to recover within 100k steps. Brackets lr: 1e-4 confirmed near-optimal.
New best (-10.4): shallower dip (-24) and smooth near-monotonic recovery; larger batches cut gradient variance. Still rising at cutoff.
Best so far and only positive run (+0.43): shallow dip then strictly monotonic climb through the whole second half, still rising steeply at cutoff. Batch settled at 128.
New best (+4.33): gamma 0.999 stacks with batch 128 — crossed zero earlier and climbed steadily; longer credit-assignment horizon pays off in Boxing's ~1800-step episodes.
Worse than Exp6 (-4.34 vs +4.33, only eps_end changed): cutting the exploration floor to 1% starved the replay buffer of diverse transitions and slowed learning. Residual exploration still pays off.

Worse (-8.0): doubling the exploration phase delayed the learning turnaround ~15k steps and the run ran out of budget. With Exp7 this brackets epsilon: default 0.1 fraction / 0.05 floor is the sweet spot.
MLP with identical hyperparameters to Exp6 plateaued at -28 and never recovered; flattened pixels lose all spatial structure. Controlled proof that CnnPolicy is required for Atari.

Champion config at 200k steps reached only +1.02 training / -3.2 greedy eval, losing to Exp6 (+4.8 greedy): exploration_fraction is relative, so doubling steps stretched the epsilon decay and reproduced Exp8's flaw. Best model overall: Exp6.

NOTE: batch_size manually reduced from planned 128 to 32 to cut runtime; not a clean seed-only comparison to Nick's champion. reward went -9.8 -> -30.3 (best -6.9), trend declining, ep_len ~1786. Much worse than Nick's champion (+4.33), suggesting batch=32 (not just seed) drove the drop, consistent with the group's batch-size trend.

NOTE: batch_size manually reduced from planned 128 to 64 to cut runtime. reward went -8.2 -> -18.2 (best -8.1), trend declining, ep_len ~1786. lr=3e-4 looks unstable/too high at this batch size, in line with Faly/Nick's higher-lr runs.

NOTE: batch_size manually reduced from planned 128 to 64 to cut runtime. reward went -8.2 -> -17.1 (best -8.1), trend declining, ep_len ~1786. Confounded by smaller batch, so this doesn't cleanly confirm/deny lr=5e-5 as a value on its own.
